_comment: 50 hand-written test cases for the LangGraph × Headroom benchmark. IDs in simple_lookup are chosen from the seeded range (1..2500). The mix is intentional — see plan: 10 simple / 15 filtered / 15 aggregation / 10 multi-step.

cases.prompt: Get me the full details of ticket #42.
Pull up ticket #1080 — I need to read the body.
Show me what ticket #7 says.
Look up ticket #888.
What is ticket #1500?
Give me ticket #23.
Read ticket #2100.
Show ticket #1999.
What does ticket #501 say?
Open ticket #1750.
Find all open billing tickets that mention a refund.
Search for unresolved technical tickets mentioning latency.
List up to 20 closed account tickets about SSO.
Find shipping tickets in the last 30 days that mention 'delivered' or 'lost'.
Search for any ticket mentioning 'webhook' or 'webhooks' — show 15 results.
Find resolved feature requests about export or CSV.
List 25 open tickets in the billing category.
Find technical tickets that mention 500 errors.
Find open shipping tickets about customs or international delivery.
Look for high-priority unresolved tickets about authentication.
Search closed tickets mentioning 'API' in the body, limit 10.
Find account tickets about workspace ownership transfer.
Show me 30 billing tickets that mention 'invoice'.
Find shipping tickets about 'damaged' packages.
Search feature requests about dark mode, keyboard shortcuts, or bulk export.
How many tickets are in each status? Give me the counts.
Count tickets by priority.
How many open tickets does each category have?
Count tickets by customer tier.
How many billing tickets are open right now?
Count the resolved tickets per category, highest first.
Aggregate urgent tickets by category.
How many tickets are in each status for the technical category?
Group tickets by priority within the billing category.
Count premium-tier tickets by status.
How many tickets were created in the last 7 days, broken down by category?
Aggregate enterprise-tier tickets by priority.
Count tickets per category, only for open status.
How many urgent tickets are still open?
Aggregate billing tickets by customer_tier.
Find 5 open billing tickets about refunds, then summarize the common patterns.
Look up ticket #500, then find 5 similar tickets and summarize their resolution status.
Find 10 open technical tickets about API latency, then aggregate them by priority and tell me the breakdown.
Look at the customer history for customer 42, then identify the most common category.
Find 5 feature requests about bulk export, then summarize what users want.
Search for tickets about 'webhook' failures, then count them by status.
Look up ticket #100, find 5 similar tickets (same category), and tell me how many were resolved.
Find 8 open shipping tickets about international delivery, then aggregate them by priority.
Search for premium-tier billing tickets about invoices, then categorize the complaints.
Find all urgent tickets in the last 14 days, group by category, and tell me which category has the most urgent issues.
